name: Tour of Bizkaia (GR 123): Bakio to Gaztelugatxe
name_original: Ruta de las ermitas — ida y vuelta desde Bakio
description: Hike the moderate 8.5-mile Route of the Hermitages from Bakio, exploring coastal cliffs, vineyards, and historic sites like Saint John of Ga
region_intro: Hiking around Bakio offers diverse terrain where the Cantabrian Sea meets mountainous landscapes. The region is characterized by dramatic coastal cliffs, expansive beaches, and surrounding mountains like Burgoa, Garbola, and Jata. Hikers can explore a natural mosaic of forests, txakoli vineyards, and a coastline stretching over 6 kilometers. This geographical setting provides varied elevation changes and scenic vistas for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Bakio The most popular hiking route is San Juan de Gaztelugatxe and its viewpoints, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 1 minute to complete. This route offers views of the iconic islet and the wild Cantabrian coast. Another top favourite among local hikers is Tour of Bizkaia (GR 123): Bakio to Gaztelugatxe, a moderate 8.5 miles (13.7 km) path. This trail follows the rugged coastline, connecting Bakio to the historic site of Gaztelugatxe. Local hikers also love the Jata y Jatatxiki — circular desde Bakio, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 13 minutes. Hiking around Bakio is defined by coastal paths, mountainous ascents, and routes through txakoli vineyards. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Bakio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 400 reviews. More than 4,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Bakio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Bakio?
A: Bakio offers a diverse network of nearly 40 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. These routes explore the region's dramatic coastal cliffs, expansive beaches, and surrounding mountains, as well as inland areas with txakoli vineyards.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Bakio?
A: The terrain around Bakio is highly varied, ranging from coastal paths along cliffs and beaches to mountainous ascents through forests and valleys. You'll encounter significant elevation changes, especially on routes leading to viewpoints or through the surrounding mountains like Jata. The area also features unique landscapes like txakoli vineyards.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Bakio?
A: Yes, Bakio offers several circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1325285043/jata-y-jatatxiki-circular-desde-bakio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jata y Jatatxiki — circular desde Bakio&lt;/a&gt; trail takes you through mountainous terrain, offering a comprehensive experience of the inland landscape. Another option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1327215379/gaztelugatxe-burgoa-and-matxitxako-lighthouse-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaztelugatxe, Burgoa, and Matxitxako Lighthouse Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which provides stunning coastal views.

Q: What are the best viewpoints or landmarks to see while hiking in Bakio?
A: Bakio is rich in stunning viewpoints and landmarks. A must-see is the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/275684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint John of Gaztelugatxe&lt;/a&gt;, an islet connected by a winding path and steps, offering spectacular views of the wild Cantabrian coast. Other notable spots include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/527262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matxitxako Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; and various points along the coast that provide panoramic vistas of the sea and mountains.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Bakio?
A: While many routes involve varied terrain and elevation, there are options suitable for families. Shorter, less strenuous walks can be found along the promenade or through the flatter sections of the txakoli vineyards. Always check the route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Bakio?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many of the hiking trails around Bakio. However, specific rules may apply in protected natural areas or on private land. It's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to carry waste bags. Always check local signage for any restrictions.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Bakio?
A: Bakio benefits from a warm micro-climate, making it suitable for hiking for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes, ideal for exploring. Summer can be warm, especially on exposed coastal paths, but the sea breeze often provides relief. Winter hiking is also possible, though some paths might be wet or muddy after rain.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails around Bakio?
A: Yes, Bakio offers 9 easy hiking routes. These typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, making them perfect for a leisurely stroll or for those new to hiking. You can find options that explore the coastal promenade or the charming inland areas.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Bakio?
A: The hiking trails in Bakio are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning coastal scenery, the unique experience of visiting Gaztelugatxe, and the diverse landscapes that include mountains, beaches, and vineyards.

Q: How can I access San Juan de Gaztelugatxe from Bakio?
A: San Juan de Gaztelugatxe is a short drive from Bakio. You can hike to it via routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046072989/san-juan-de-gaztelugatxe-and-its-viewpoints" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan de Gaztelugatxe and its viewpoints&lt;/a&gt;, which offers incredible views of the iconic islet. Due to its popularity, especially after being featured in 'Game of Thrones,' free tickets are required for access to this protected area, so it's advisable to book in advance.

Q: Are there public transport options to reach hiking trailheads near Bakio?
A: Bakio is a well-connected town, and public transport options are available to reach various points within the region. While specific bus routes to every trailhead might vary, local buses can often get you close to popular starting points. It's recommended to check local bus schedules for the most up-to-date information on routes and timings.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Bakio?
A: Parking is generally available in Bakio, particularly near the town center and popular attractions. For specific trailheads, designated parking areas may exist, especially for well-known routes like those leading towards Gaztelugatxe. It's advisable to arrive early during peak season to secure a spot.
tips: At the tip of the port breakwater, right next to Bakio beach, you will find this eight-meter work by José Ramón Anda, representing the profile of a sloop with sails in the wind.

Bakio beach is ideal for surfing. The waves are of a high quality and suitable for surfers of all levels. In addition, it has a long promenade with extraordinary views that runs through it completely and is surrounded by all kinds of services.
very nice vantage point with tables and benches

Although the hermitage of San Juan de Gaztelugatxe and its stairway have adorned this spectacular islet since the 10th century, it was not until the place appeared as a setting in the Game of Thrones television series that it gained great popularity. So much so, that it is necessary to get free tickets to access. The ascent to the hermitage consists of 241 steps and although the climb is arduous, it is well worth it. The views of the wild Cantabrian coast and the beauty of the isolate, considered a biotope, with its hermitage on top offer you an unforgettable experience.

This small Romanesque gem is one of the few examples of this art that you will find in Bizkaia.
The 12th century hermitage is also located in a privileged location, very close to the town of Bakio, but also to San Juan de Gaztelugatxe and Cape Matxitxaco.

faqs.question: Where does the Route of the Hermitages trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts and ends in the picturesque municipality of Bakio. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Bakio is a coastal town, so you can generally find parking options within the town to begin your hike. Public transport to Bakio is also an option, connecting you to the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is the Route of the Hermitages — round trip from Bakio suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is generally dog-friendly. However, a significant portion of the trail passes through the Protected Biotope of Gaztelugatxe. It's crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times within this protected area to respect wildlife and other hikers. Always ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Route of the Hermitages?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is generally during spring (April-June) and early autumn (September-October). During these months, the weather is usually pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the potentially wetter, cooler conditions of winter. This allows for comfortable hiking and clear views of the stunning coastal landscapes.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Route of the Hermitages, especially for the Protected Biotope of Gaztelugatxe?
faqs.answer: There is no general entrance fee or permit required to hike the Route of the Hermitages itself. However, as parts of the trail traverse the Protected Biotope of Gaztelugatxe, it's important to be aware of and respect the environmental regulations of this protected area. These typically include staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife, and leaving no trace. For specific access to Saint John of Gaztelugatxe, especially during peak seasons, you might need to reserve a time slot, though this is usually for direct access to the islet, not the hiking trail itself.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hiking route?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly accessible paths, but some sections require sure-footedness. You'll encounter a diverse mix of coastal paths, inclines and declines along cliffs, and potentially some forested or vineyard areas as it traverses the verdant landscapes around Bakio. The trail is classified as moderate, so expect varied surfaces.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Route of the Hermitages?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in natural beauty and cultural heritage. Key highlights include the Belak Sculpture at Bakio Beach, Bakio Beach itself, and the breathtaking View of San Juan de Gaztelugatxe. You'll also encounter the iconic Saint John of Gaztelugatxe and the Hermitage of San Pelayo de Bakio, among other historic hermitages and stunning coastal vistas.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with any other well-known hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other official trails. You'll find sections that overlap with the Bizkaiko bira, Gibelortzaga (San Pelaio), Birjilandako mailak, and the Gr123, offering opportunities to explore further if you wish to extend your hike.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Bakio, where you'll find various amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and restrooms. Along the route itself, especially in more remote coastal or natural sections, facilities will be limited. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, and use facilities in Bakio before you set off.

faqs.question: Is the Route of the Hermitages suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and approximately 13.7 km length, the full round trip might be challenging for very young children. However, families with older, active children who are used to hiking should find it enjoyable. There are many accessible paths, and you can always choose to do shorter sections of the route to enjoy the coastal views and hermitages.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate coastal hike, you should bring sturdy hiking shoes, appropriate clothing for the weather (layers are recommended), sun protection (hat, sunscreen), plenty of water, and some snacks. A camera is a must for the stunning views, and binoculars can enhance wildlife spotting. Don't forget a fully charged phone for navigation and emergencies.

faqs.question: Are there any specific cultural traditions or unique experiences associated with the hermitages on this route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The most famous tradition is at Saint John of Gaztelugatxe. After climbing the 241 steps to the hermitage, it's customary to ring the bell three times and make a wish. This spiritual and historic site is a major highlight and offers a unique cultural experience.
